senses.definition: (in sports) the players who try to not allow the opposing team to score; the position or role of these players on the field
a defensive soccer player positioned behind the main defenders to provide additional cover and clearances

examples.x: The sweeper intercepted the through ball before it reached the striker.
The team relies on their sweeper to organize the defense and prevent counterattacks.
His role as a sweeper requires quick decision-making and precise tackles.
The sweeper's clearance saved his team from conceding a goal.
She played as a sweeper throughout her career, excelling in reading the game.

senses.definition: the four defensive players, in a soccer match, whose main objective is to stop the opposing team from scoring goals

examples.x: The coach decided to reinforce the back four to maintain a solid defense against the opponent's attacking lineup.
With the back four in position, the team aimed to thwart any attempts at goal from their rivals.
The opposing team struggled to break through the back four's resilient defense, resulting in a stalemate.
The goalkeeper relied on clear communication with the back four to organize the team's defensive strategy effectively.
Despite facing relentless pressure, the back four held their ground admirably throughout the match, ensuring a clean sheet for their team.

senses.definition: (American football) a defensive lineman who lines up in the center of the defensive line to stop the run and disrupt the offense

examples.x: The nose tackle clogged up the middle, preventing the running back from gaining any yardage.
Our team's nose tackle is known for his incredible strength and ability to shed blockers.
In a 3-4 defense, the nose tackle plays a crucial role in anchoring the defensive line.
The opposing quarterback struggled all game due to the relentless pressure from our nose tackle.
The nose tackle's job is to occupy blockers, allowing the linebackers to make tackles.

senses.definition: a football player who defends against the run and rushes the quarterback

examples.x: The defensive end sacked the quarterback for a loss of ten yards.
Our team's defensive end is known for his speed off the edge.
He plays defensive end with a relentless pursuit of the ball carrier.
The coach praised the defensive end for his disciplined play.
The defensive end forced a fumble with a powerful tackle.

senses.definition: (American football) a defensive player who stops the run, covers pass receivers, and strike the quarterback when needed

examples.x: The linebacker made a tackle for a loss on the running back.
The linebacker intercepted a pass and returned it for a touchdown.
As a linebacker, he fills gaps to stop the run and drops back into coverage on passing downs.
With a powerful hit, the linebacker forced a fumble that was recovered by his team.

senses.definition: (American football) a defensive player positioned in the center of the field, responsible for coordinating and leading the defense

examples.x: The middle linebacker called out defensive adjustments before the snap.
He is known for his hard hits and leadership as a middle linebacker.
The middle linebacker intercepted the pass and returned it for a touchdown.
She was voted the team's MVP for her stellar season as middle linebacker.
His speed and agility make him an effective middle linebacker in coverage.

senses.definition: a defensive player in American football who primarily defends against the run and pass from the outer edges of the defensive formation

examples.x: The outside linebacker blitzed the quarterback from the edge of the formation.
The team's outside linebacker intercepted a pass and returned it for a touchdown.
He is known for his speed and agility, ideal traits for an outside linebacker.
Outside linebackers must be versatile, capable of defending against both the run and the pass.
Teams often use their outside linebackers to disrupt the opposing team's passing game.

senses.definition: a defensive player in American football who covers wide receivers to prevent them from catching passes and defends against deep throws

examples.x: As a cornerback, he excels in man-to-man coverage and zone defense strategies.
The cornerback intercepted a pass in the end zone to stop a scoring drive.
He's known as the team's top cornerback, adept at breaking up passes.
With precise footwork, the cornerback mirrored the receiver's route and forced an incomplete pass.
The cornerback's speed allowed him to close the gap and tackle the receiver immediately after the catch.

senses.definition: a defensive back in American football who is the fifth defensive back, often used in passing situations
a defensive player in American football used primarily in situations requiring extra pass coverage

examples.x: In the third quarter, the dimeback intercepted a crucial pass.
The defense added a dimeback to handle the speedy wide receivers.
Coordinating with the nickelback, the dimeback ensured the secondary was airtight.
The coach decided to bring in the dimeback to counter the opponent's passing attack.

senses.definition: a player in football responsible for preventing the opposing team from completing forward passes and making significant gains

examples.x: The defensive back intercepted the pass and ran it back for a touchdown.
Our team's defensive back made a crucial tackle in the final minutes.
He was praised for his skills as a defensive back after the game.
The defensive back positioned himself perfectly to break up the pass.
Training camp focused heavily on improving the defensive backs' techniques.

senses.definition: a player who is primarily responsible for defending their team's goal or territory, and preventing the opposing team from scoring
a player that guards the goal in soccer or other sports

examples.x: The goalkeeper made a spectacular save to keep the match tied.
As the last line of defense, the goalkeeper plays a crucial role in the team's success.
The goalkeeper dove to his left to block the penalty kick.
She trained rigorously to improve her skills as a goalkeeper.
The goalkeeper's quick reflexes earned him the player of the match award.

senses.definition: the player who throws the ball toward home plate, aiming to get batters out and prevent runs

examples.x: The pitcher struck out the batter with a fastball on the outside corner.
He's the team's ace pitcher, known for his precise control and variety of pitches.
With runners on base, the pitcher induced a ground ball for a double play.
The pitcher's ability to change speeds effectively makes him difficult to hit.

senses.definition: a defensive player in baseball who fields positions within the diamond, including first base, second base, shortstop, and third base

examples.x: The infielder quickly threw to first base for the out.
The infielder turned a smooth double play to end the inning.
The infielder made a diving stop to prevent a hit.
With a quick reaction, the infielder fielded a hard ground ball.
He's a versatile infielder, capable of playing both shortstop and second base.

senses.definition: a defensive player in baseball who fields positions in the outfield, including left field, center field, and right field

examples.x: The outfielder's speed allows him to cover a lot of ground.
The outfielder made a spectacular catch at the wall.
He's a strong-armed outfielder who prevents extra-base hits.
The outfielder threw out a runner trying to score from third base.
Positioned in center field, the outfielder tracked down a deep fly ball.

senses.definition: a cricket player positioned on the boundary or deep in the field who catches high balls and stops runs

examples.x: He's an excellent outfielder known for his strong throwing arm.
The outfielder stopped the ball from reaching the boundary.
Positioned deep, the outfielder threw the ball quickly to the wicketkeeper.
The outfielder's speed allows him to cover a lot of ground.
With a diving effort, the outfielder saved a certain boundary.

senses.definition: (baseball) an infielder who plays near first base and catches throws to get batters out

examples.x: The first baseman made a quick stretch to catch the throw and get the runner out.
He's the team's first baseman, known for his strong defensive skills.
The first baseman's height helps him catch high throws from other infielders.
The first baseman's quick reflexes saved an errant throw from going into the outfield.
The first baseman caught a line drive hit down the first base line.

senses.definition: a defensive player positioned on the left side of the field or court in field hockey and soccer

examples.x: Our left half played an exceptional game, blocking multiple attacks.
She was moved to the left half to strengthen our defense.
The left half coordinated well with the center back to stop the opposing team.
As a left half, he excelled at marking the opposing winger.
The coach praised the left half for his consistent performance.

senses.definition: a versatile defensive player in soccer, often acting as a sweeper, positioned behind the main line of defense

examples.x: The libero cleared the ball off the goal line in a crucial moment.
The coach assigned the experienced player to the libero position.
Known for his vision and tactical awareness, the libero directed the defense flawlessly.
Playing as a libero requires excellent reading of the game and anticipation.
The libero communicated constantly with the goalkeeper and defenders.

senses.definition: a volleyball player who specializes in quick attacks and blocking in the center of the net

examples.x: The middle hitter blocked the opposing team's spike with ease.
Our team's middle hitter is known for her quick reflexes at the net.
As a middle hitter, his primary role is to dominate the net with quick hits.
She trained hard to become the team's top middle hitter this season.
During the match, the middle hitter scored several points with her powerful attacks.

senses.definition: a key player in soccer responsible for both defending and attacking, often positioned in the middle of the field

examples.x: The central midfielder intercepted the ball and initiated a counter-attack.
Our central midfielder consistently disrupts the opponent’s play.
The coach praised the central midfielder for excellent ball distribution.
She excelled as a central midfielder, balancing defense and offense.
He’s known as the best central midfielder in the league.

senses.definition: a soccer player positioned centrally who primarily focuses on defending and breaking up the opposition's attacks

examples.x: As a central defensive midfielder, her job is to shield the defense.
The coach praised the central defensive midfielder for his positioning.
Our central defensive midfielder has great vision and anticipation.

senses.definition: (volleyball) a player known for exceptional defensive skills, particularly in receiving serves and digging balls

examples.x: She's the best defensive specialist in the league, with the most digs per game.
The defensive specialist made a crucial dig to keep the rally alive.
Our team relies on our defensive specialist to handle tough serves.
The coach praised the defensive specialist for her consistent passing under pressure.
As a defensive specialist, his focus is on perfecting his passing technique.

senses.definition: a player in American football who prevents defensive players from tackling the ball carrier or quarterback

examples.x: He's a strong blocker on the offensive line.
The blocker picked up a blitzing linebacker.
The blocker opened a lane for the running back to score.
Positioned at guard, the blocker protected the quarterback.
The blocker's job is crucial for successful running plays.

senses.definition: a player in ice hockey, whose primary role is to defend their team's goal and prevent the opposing team from scoring

examples.x: The blueliner's exceptional defensive skills made him a formidable opponent on the ice.
With lightning-fast reflexes, the blueliner intercepted passes and blocked shots, frustrating the opposing team's offensive efforts.
Known for his physicality and hockey smarts, the blueliner anchored the team's defense and provided stability in critical moments.
The coach relied on the blueliner to shut down the opposing team's top scorers and maintain a strong defensive presence throughout the game.
In a tight match, the blueliner's crucial block in the final minutes preserved the team's lead and secured the victory.

senses.definition: (basketball) the defensive players responsible for guarding the opposing team's guards

examples.x: Jones's quick hands disrupted the opponent's backcourt passing lanes.
The backcourt pressured the opposing guards into multiple turnovers.
The team's backcourt struggled with foul trouble early in the game.
The backcourt successfully contained the opponent's fast breaks throughout the game.
The coach credited the backcourt for their tenacity in closing out on shooters.

senses.definition: a defensive player in American football who defends against long passes and provides extra coverage in the secondary

examples.x: The free safety intercepted the quarterback's pass and returned it for a touchdown.
The free safety's primary responsibility is to prevent deep passes and support the cornerbacks.
The free safety adjusted his coverage based on the offensive formation.
Our team's free safety is known for his ability to read the quarterback's eyes.
Despite the offense's efforts, the free safety broke up the pass intended for the wide receiver.
